chapter_context.summary: Presents a highly detailed prophetic history of the geopolitical conflicts between the King of the South (Ptolemies) and the King of the North (Seleucids), leading to the rise of a contemptible king who pollutes the sanctuary, sets up the abomination, and exalts himself above all gods.

outline.text: Persian Empire Conflicts and the Conquests of Alexander the Great
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Persian Empire Conflicts and the Conquests of Alexander the Great (11:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Detailed Prophecy of Intrigues Between Kings of the South and North
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Detailed Prophecy of Intrigues Between Kings of the South and North (11:5-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: The Rise of the Despicable King (Antiochus IV) and Desecration of Sanctuary
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Rise of the Despicable King (Antiochus IV) and Desecration of Sanctuary (11:21-35), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: The Self-Magnifying King and His Ultimate Destruction
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Self-Magnifying King and His Ultimate Destruction (11:36-45), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

key_themes.title: The Sovereignty of God Over Kingdoms
key_themes.description: A central message of Daniel is that God rules in the kingdom of men, setting up and deposing kings according to His eternal purpose.

key_themes.title: Faithfulness under Pressure
key_themes.description: Daniel and his friends demonstrate unwavering allegiance to God's law in the face of compromise, death, and exile.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the faithfulness and courage demonstrated in Chapter 11, how can you stand firm in your convictions when faced with cultural and societal pressures to compromise?
In what ways does the vision of God's ultimate eternal kingdom in this chapter bring hope and stability to your daily life?

cultural_historical.title: Babylonian and Persian Courts
cultural_historical.text: Daniel was trained in the literature and language of the Chaldeans, serving as a high-ranking official under both Babylonian and Medo-Persian administrations, illustrating the historical reality of the Jewish diaspora.
